reviews.review_msg: Tim is a good person but does expect students to have prior knowledge despite no prerq for this course. Dont recommend unless mandatory. All info dumped on slides + no clear learning outcomes provided which makes studying for tests hell. He curved first midterm but class average was not great. Final was tough with majority written questions.

Lectures not very engaging, and slides are often filled to the brim. Over 50% of the course failed the first midterm, so he raised the grades up by 30%, and took student feedback for how the next midterm should be formatted. I had a great TA as well. Lots of reading and details, so get ready for that!

This course covers a lot of content in a lot of detail and exams, as expected, are super specific. Knowledge of specific biological terms is a must. First exam was curved heavily because of low average. Dr. Beischlag is responsive to student needs and changed the format of the next exam which was really appreciated !
lectures so boring and heavy, he doesnt really care about students and gives no exam prep or guidance. 3 midterms each 28% with questions about random details. do not recommend him

Slides were just filled to the max. Two or three chapters assigned most weeks. Multiple choice exams based on very specific details rather than concepts. no guidance for studying, prof read lectures off from the ppt. Memorizing slides barely helps. Flashcards also wont help, just so much disorganized info. Memorize textbook + lecture

I had Dr. Beischlag for my previous HSCI course, and I enjoyed both classes. My final class with him was much more relaxing compared to the other. We had three group presentations and received good grades as long as you put in the effort. He's always available for help and loves to make jokes in class too. Don't be afraid to speak with him!

Very clear at explaining the lecture slides. Willing to answer questions at any time and approachable. Older lectures are available online but going to class can help. 3 exams were totally fair! Would take another course with him!

Class is based on lectures. There are only 3 midterms in the semester, which are not cumulative, so it's pretty easy. They were online and open book.  As long as you read through the slides, your good. Lots of memorization.

Dr. Beischlag makes lectures engaging. He uses analogies that are easy to understand. Exams are very straightforward and mainly based on lecture recordings. However, the textbook provides good info for reference or simply a better understanding of the concepts. There is minimal communication with him directly, but the TA's are very responsive.

This class is based on 3 exams that come directly out of the lecture slides, reading is important to complete your understanding but no material will be tested that was not mentioned in lecture. His teaching skills are pretty average, but enthusiastic :P He genuinely wants you to succeed in his class and he is very accommodating .
